signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Macro Divergence
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4355 (implied) — NZD outperformance vs CAD weakness creates a 1.551% G8 spread, the widest in the complex. NZD is supported by a hawkish RBNZ bias (hike priced for 27 May) while CAD faces pressure from softer April employment data and an on-hold BoC. The +0bp carry neutralizes the rate advantage, but the policy divergence drives the trend. Watch for the RBNZ decision on 27 May to confirm the long-term decoupling.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7246 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates institutional conviction, while retail sentiment is at an extreme 88% short. This 76-point divergence suggests retail is aggressively fading the trend, increasing the risk of a short-squeeze if price holds above 0.7250. RBA at 4.35% (HIKING) vs Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) provides a -60bp AUD carry premium supporting the upside. Watch for retail capitulation to accelerate the move toward 0.7300.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Headline + Correlation Break
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1780 — ECB's Lagarde warns that climbing energy costs from Strait of Hormuz tensions will push up input prices, keeping hikes in focus despite the current hold. EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation has shifted to +0.720 (z=+1.78σ), breaking the historical norm of -0.125. This indicates EUR is trading as a 'risk-on' proxy for European equity recovery rather than a safe-haven. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% maintains a +175bp spread favoring USD, capping upside unless energy shocks force an ECB pivot.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.65 — Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% provides a +300bp carry advantage that sustains the bid despite VIX at 17.3 (normal volatility). JPY COT LF net short -88,543 remains crowded, leaving the pair vulnerable to rapid unwinds if geopolitical tensions in the Middle East escalate further. 160.00 remains the critical watch level for BoJ verbal or physical intervention. Carry dominates the regime until VIX exceeds 25.
